Identification.LongTitle: An Act to prohibit fur farming

Provision.Text: This enactment establishes prohibitions and offences for certain activities involving fur farming.
Her Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate and House of Commons of Canada, enacts as follows:

Section.Text: This Act may be cited as the Prohibition of Fur Farming Act.
The following definitions apply in this Act.

Definition.Text: fur farmâmeans a place where fur-bearing animals are kept in captivity in order for their pelt to be used for commercial purposes. (ferme dâÃ©levage dâanimaux Ã fourrure)
Ministerâmeans the Minister of Agriculture and Agri-Food. (ministre)

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: own or operate a fur farm or cause a fur farm to be operated;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: breed or inseminate a fur-bearing animal for the purpose of sending it or its offspring to a fur farm;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: slaughter a fur-bearing animal to use its pelt for commercial purposes; or

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: give, rent, sell or transport a fur-bearing animal to a fur farm.

Section.Text: Every person who contravenes section 3 is guilty of an offence and liable

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: on conviction on indictment, to a fine of not more than $250,000 or to imprisonment for a term of not more than two years, or to both; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: on summary conviction, to a fine of not more than $50,000 or to imprisonment for a term of not more than six months, or to both.

Section.Text: The court may, in addition to any other punishment that may be imposed under section 4, make an order

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: prohibiting the accused from owning, having custody or control of, or residing in the same premises as an animal during any period that the court considers appropriate; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: on application of the Attorney General or on its own motion, directing the accused to pay to a person or an organization that has taken care of a fur-bearing animal as a result of the commission of the offence the reasonable costs that the person or organization incurred in respect of the fur-bearing animal.

Section.Text: On ex parte application by the Attorney General, a provincial court judge as defined in section 2 of the Criminal Code or a judge as defined in subsection 462.3(1) of that Act may order that a fur-bearing animal be forfeited to Her Majesty in right of Canada.

Section.MarginalNote: Forfeiture â if person found guilty
Section.Text: If a person is found guilty of an offence under section 4, the court may, in addition to any other punishment imposed under that section, order that anything by means of which or in respect of which the offence was committed be forfeited to Her Majesty in right of Canada.

Subsection.Text: Anything that is forfeited under section 6 or 7 must be disposed of as the Minister directs.
The Minister must give priority to transferring any fur-bearing animal forfeited to an animal sanctuary, shelter or rescue service.

Section.Text: The Governor in Council may make regulations in respect of a regime that provides compensation for losses incurred as a result of complying with this Act.
This Act comes into force one year after the day on which it receives royal assent.
